name: Loop with view on the Gave
name_original: Boucle avec vue sur le Gave
region_intro: Road cycling around Sauveterre-De-Béarn features varied terrain, ranging from gentle routes along the Gave d'Oloron river to more challenging ascents in the surrounding Béarn countryside. The region is characterized by its rolling hills and offers distant views of the Pyrenees mountain range. Cyclists can explore routes that traverse agricultural lands and follow river valleys. Best road cycling routes around Sauveterre-De-Béarn The most popular road cycling route is Loop with view on the Gave, an easy 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Gave d'Oloron river. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of the Adour River – Berge de l'adour🤩 loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren, a difficult 127.8 miles (205.6 km) path. This route features extensive views of the Adour River and its banks. Local road cyclists also love the Abbaye de Sordes loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren, a 39.4 miles (63.3 km) trail leading through the Béarn countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Sauveterre-De-Béarn is defined by the Gave d'Oloron river, the verdant Béarn countryside, and distant Pyrenees mountains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to difficult long-distance rides. The routes in Sauveterre-De-Béarn are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 250 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sauveterre-De-Béarn's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sauveterre-de-Béarn?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Sauveterre-de-Béarn. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What is the terrain like for road cycling in Sauveterre-de-Béarn?
A: The terrain around Sauveterre-de-Béarn is varied, featuring rolling hills and routes that traverse agricultural lands and follow river valleys. You'll find everything from gentle rides along the Gave d'Oloron river to more challenging ascents in the surrounding Béarn countryside, often with distant views of the Pyrenees mountains.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Sauveterre-de-Béarn?
A: The road cycling routes in Sauveterre-de-Béarn are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain and scenic beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for beginners. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e852253031/loop-with-view-on-the-gave" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop with view on the Gave&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25.4 km trail that offers pleasant views of the Gave d'Oloron river.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in the region?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy the picturesque views of the Gave d'Oloron river, the verdant Béarn countryside, and distant panoramas of the Pyrenees mountains. The region is also home to the Glère Island, a classified Sensitive Natural Area along the river.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the routes?
A: Absolutely. Sauveterre-de-Béarn is rich in history. You can encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3402827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de La Légende&lt;/a&gt;, a listed Historical Monument offering stunning views, and the imposing 12th-century Monréal Tower. The Saint-André Church, dating back to the 12th and 13th centuries, is also a notable sight.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37684707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Adour River – Berge de l'adour🤩 loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 205.6 km path featuring extensive views and significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Sauveterre-de-Béarn?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15012728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Sordes loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren&lt;/a&gt; is a 63.3 km trail that takes you through the Béarn countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sauveterre-de-Béarn?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions throughout much of the year due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with comfortable temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it's advisable to cycle earlier in the morning to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through other towns or offer connections to other areas?
A: Yes, some routes extend beyond Sauveterre-de-Béarn, connecting to other charming towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39373865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orthez – Le Pont-Vieux (Orthez) loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren&lt;/a&gt; takes you towards Orthez, offering a chance to explore more of the Béarn region.

Q: Can I find routes that are longer distances for endurance training?
A: For longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37684707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Adour River – Berge de l'adour🤩 loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 205.6 km route, perfect for endurance training and experiencing extensive views of the Adour River.

Q: Are there any routes that feature specific historical sites like abbeys?
A: Yes, you can cycle routes that lead to historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15012728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Sordes loop from Autevielle-Saint-Martin-Bideren&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, guiding you through the countryside towards the historic Abbaye de Sordes.
tips:
Sauveterre is a “Castral Rescue”. From its origin in the 11th century, the city has been a place of refuge, it welcomes and protects the population by placing it under the direct protection of the viscount.

It was not until the reign of two great viscounts of Béarn (Gaston VII Moncade and Gaston Fébus) in the 13th and 14th centuries to see the city counted among the four main boroughs of Béarn. The pilgrims of Compostela from the year 1000 will also participate in the enrichment of this city on the way to Vézelay.

Located at the gates of Béarn, the city is endowed with impressive ramparts and defensive sites. Bridge, Tour Monréal, church, arsenal…, for the most part built on a rocky pile. Who would dare to blame Sauveterre?

It was in the 16th century that this beautiful story came to an end. Attacks by the Spaniards and wars of religion got the better of the city despite its impressive ramparts and thus ended Sauveterre's strategic role at the gates of Béarn.

Source: https://www.tourisme-bearn-gaves.com/
The Gave d'Oloron is the meeting of the Gave d'Aspe and the Gave d'Ossau. A nice view especially when the sun goes down!
Excellent address, whether you are looking for a place to eat, buy or taste very good artisanal charcuterie, the place is great.
